title: herniaria hirsuta subsp. cinerea
intro: Herniaria hirsuta subsp. cinerea, also known as hairy sweet-scented cinquefoil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic. Herniaria hirsuta subsp. cinerea can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, and its leaves and roots can be used in various herbal preparations, including salves and poultices.
